candidates.title: Thrilling Thursday: Meet Reptiles with Extant Relatives
candidates.description: Extant Relatives is a local evidence-based, community-friendly herpetology group offering the chance to interact with charismatic and well-socialized reptiles and amphibians in a way that is safe for everyone. Their goal is for everyone to understand herps through the life history, evolution, and ecology of the species. Come learn about all about their incredible collection of reptiles! Join us for a Thrilling Thursday program at 10am or 1pm every Thursday in the summer! 欢迎参加暑假期间每周四上午10点和下午1点举办的“精彩星期四”（Thrilling Thursday）活动！ “Extant Relatives”（现存亲属）是一个立足本地社区的两栖爬行动物科学社团。他们致力于让人们有机会在确保安全的前提下，与那些性格温顺且适应人类接触的两栖爬行动物近距离互动。他们的目标是让大家通过了解这些物种的生活史、进化历程及生态习性，来认识两栖爬行动物。一起来观赏这些令人惊叹的爬行动物吧！

candidates.title: Play Daze at Calder Park
candidates.description: Summer PLAY DAZE are here! Enjoy visiting the ICPL Bookmobile, & activities provided by Iowa City Parks and Recreation including: free arts, crafts, theatre, games, nature experiments, and STEAM fun. Sensory tent available! Activities presented by Iowa City Parks and Recreation run from 9:30-11:30am on Thursdays this summer. The ICPL Bookmobile will be present from 10-11am each week. More details can be found on the City of Iowa City webpage .

candidates.title: ICPL Book Bike hosted by ICPL Friends Foundation
candidates.description: Connect with the Iowa City Public Library Friends Foundation, whose mission is to strengthen ICPL through fundraising, advocacy, and promotion of library resources. Join us outside the College Street entrance to the Iowa City Public Library. Whether you’re a longtime library user or just curious about what’s inside, this is a great opportunity to learn more about the ICPL Friends Foundation, ICPLFF volunteer roles, and giving opportunities. Interested in learning more about the Iowa City Public Library? Come inside for information about our services and programs. From our diverse book and media collections—including audiobooks, DVDs, and multilingual materials—to our digital resources like eBooks, streaming services, and research databases, there’s something for everyone. Katie Roche, Director of the Iowa City Public Library Friends Foundation, will be on hand to chat, answer questions, and share ways to get involved and support the Library's mission. And don’t leave empty-handed—pick out a free book to take home, yours to keep as a gift from the ICPL Friends Foundation! In case of rain, event will be cancelled. We can’t wait to see you!

candidates.title: Iowa City Math Circle Summer Camp - Math 1
candidates.description: The Iowa City Math Circle has been enriching students for 8 years. We are holding a free Math Camp for middle schoolers and high schoolers interested in preparing for math competitions, specifically AMC and AIME, as well as math beyond the school curriculum. We recommend a prerequisite of at least Algebra 1 to understand the material. Camp Schedule: Jul 6 - Jul 10: Math 1 Jul 13 - Jul 17: Math 2 Jul 20 - Jul 24: Coding 1 Jul 27 - Jul 31: Coding 2 Registration is required. Go to Iowa City Math Circle to register or if you have any questions. Snacks will be provided during every camp session. Join our instructors for lots of fun and learning this summer! Sponsored by the Iowa City Math Circle and Iowa City Public Library Teen Services.

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Curious Minds with Charlotte
candidates.description: Why do we think the way we do? What influences our ethical compass? Charlotte is here to walk us through our curious minds. This program is aimed at increasing cognitive ability, enhancing social skills, and supporting emotional well-being. Charlotte Hilker is a speech-language pathologist and research associate at the University of Iowa. She holds a bachelor's degree in Philosophy and is an alumna of the US Fulbright Program in Kosovo. She is passionate about bringing philosophy to the classroom in fun and engaging ways!

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Disney Bingo
candidates.description: This is just a chill game where it pays to be lucky.

candidates.title: Totally Tweens: Learn Card Tricks with Angie
candidates.description: Miss Angie, Children's Department Coordinator, has been a fan of magic tricks, especially card tricks, since she was a kid. Today, she will teach us a few simple, guaranteed-to-work magic card tricks you can use to amaze your friends and family! A deck of cards will be provided to each participant. This event is for kids in grades 3-6. 儿童服务部Angie从小就喜爱魔术，尤其是纸牌魔术。今天，她将教大家几个简单易学、保证成功的纸牌魔术，让你可以向亲朋好友大显身手！本活动面向小学三至六年级的儿童，将为每位参与者提供一副扑克牌。

candidates.title: Shortened Help Hours in the Digital Media Lab
candidates.description: Note: Help Hours will end early at 6:30 to make space for Very Basic Video Editing. Want some help starting a digital media project like scanning slides, editing a video, or converting a VHS tape? Expect to have questions while you use Adobe software? During Help Hours, a staff member can help you get started with a project, problem-solve struggles with our software and hardware, or just show you what's available in the Digital Media Lab. The Cricut, Elgato digital converters, and 8mm film converter can be reserved during this session. Go to icpl.org/DML to check availability and make your reservation. The inkjet printer is also available for drop-in printing. Note: Ordinary tech questions about using your devices or navigating a website can be asked anytime at the 2nd floor Info Desk.

From June 11 through August 13, weekly on Thursdays from 6:30-8:00pm, enjoy live music, visits from the ICPL Bookmobile, and outdoor recreation activities at some of your favorite Iowa City Parks. More details can be found on the City of Iowa City webpage .

candidates.title: Very Basic Video Editing
candidates.description: Ready for the next step beyond the simplest edits your phone can do with your videos? In this basics class, we'll use Adobe Premiere Pro to remove footage in the middle of a video. This is helpful for your own videos with boring parts as well as digitized VHS tapes or film footage you've created in the Digital Media Lab. This class is ideal for people who are comfortable using a computer but new to Premiere Pro. We'll look at the software's layout, learn how to load a video, navigate to specific points in the footage, and remove sections at the beginning, middle, and end of the video. After the hour-long session, you'll be welcome to stick around and continue practicing or editing one of your own videos. Join me for a supportive environment where no question is too stupid! Please register, as space is limited. If you have your own laptop with Adobe Premiere Pro on it, you're welcome to register for the reminder email only. Instructor: Stacey, DML manager

candidates.title: Stories in the Park at Mercer Park
candidates.description: Join us for Stories in the Park all summer long! Note the early Stories in the Park time at 9:30 to beat the midmorning heat! We will offer the same Storytime over the course of the week, so please choose one park location a week to attend. We will be at Willow Creek park on Mondays, Wetherby Park on Wednesdays, Mercer Park on Fridays. Fridays are in Mercer Park, near the playground equipment. The ICPL bookmobile will be on site and open for checkout. Library cards can be issued and updated. In the event of rain, the program will be canceled but the bookmobile will still be on site. 暑期公园故事会将于每周一，三，五上午9：30 举行，周一的活动地点在Willow Creek 公园，周三在 Wetherby 公园，周五在 Mercer 公园。不同地点每周的活动内容基本一样，请选择一个方便的公园参加。 周五的故事会将在Mercer公园的游乐场旁边举行。 ICPL 图书车也将来到现场为您提供开卡和借书服务。如遇雨天，故事会将取消，图书车仍照常服务。

candidates.title: Summer Storytime: Tippi Toes
candidates.description: Today a special guest from Tippi Toes will offer a unique program of dance and creative movement. Tippi Toes provides children with a fun, positive and nurturing environment so they are able to experience the joys of dance, self-expression and movement. Classes are specifically designed to develop motor skills and body awareness while enhancing each child’s self-esteem. Join us in the Storytime Room for Summer Storytime on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday and Saturday mornings at 10:30am. The Storytime librarian will vary each week, and every day is open to all ages! Catch the all-ages Thrilling Thursday programs 10:00am in Meeting Room A. This is additional Storytime option to Stories in the Park at 9:30am on Mondays at Willow Creek Park, Wednesdays at Wetherby Park, and Fridays at Mercer Park! 暑期故事会将于每周一，二， 三，五, 六上午10：30在图书馆儿童活动室举行。 每周将由不同的工作人员带来不同的活动内容，欢迎所有年龄段的朋友参与！每周四上午10:00 将在会议室A举办Thrilling Thursday周四特别活动。 此外，图书馆还将于每周一，三，五上午9：30举办公园故事会，周一上午活动地点在 Willow Creek Park，周三在 Wetherby Park， 周五在 Mercer Park。 Únete a nosotros en la Sala de Cuentos para la Hora del Cuento de Verano los lunes, martes, miércoles y viernes por la mañana a las 10:30 am. ¡La bibliotecaria de la Hora del Cuento variará cada semana y todos los días están abiertos para todas las edades! ¡No te pierdas los emocionantes programas para todas las edades los jueves en un nuevo horario, a las 10:00 am en la Sala de Reuniones A! ¡Esta es una opción adicional para la Hora del Cuento en el Parque a las 9:30 am lo

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Digital Paint-along with Stacey
candidates.description: Everyone will sit at a computer for this digital paint-along and create the same scene using Kleki, a free website with tools very similar to Photoshop, Procreate, and other digital painting software. You'll learn how to use brushes, paint colors, layers, and other effects to create digital art -- and you'll leave with an image file of your own masterpiece!

candidates.title: Iowa City Math Circle Summer Camp - Math 1
candidates.description: The Iowa City Math Circle has been enriching students for 8 years. We are holding a free Math Camp for middle schoolers and high schoolers interested in preparing for math competitions, specifically AMC and AIME, as well as math beyond the school curriculum. We recommend a prerequisite of at least Algebra 1 to understand the material. Camp Schedule: Jul 6 - Jul 10: Math 1 Jul 13 - Jul 17: Math 2 Jul 20 - Jul 24: Coding 1 Jul 27 - Jul 31: Coding 2 Registration is required. Go to Iowa City Math Circle to register or if you have any questions. Snacks will be provided during every camp session. Join our instructors for lots of fun and learning this summer! Sponsored by the Iowa City Math Circle and Iowa City Public Library Teen Services.

candidates.title: Teen Summer: Johnson County Conservation Reptile Program
candidates.description: YES interns and staff will be on hand to share live reptiles and answer your questions. The Johnson County Conservation Department strives to provide county residents access to the highest quality healthy natural areas, outdoor recreation opportunities and conservation education. These services are provided at more than 18 sites throughout Johnson County including natural areas, parks, preserves, and trails. The Conservation Department headquarters are located at the 1,062 acre F.W. Kent Park, with additional operations centers located at Cangleska Wakan, Pechman Creek Delta, and Sutliff.

candidates.title: Friday Flick: Toy Story
candidates.description: Take a break from the summer sun and join us every Friday for a matinee movie showing! An astonishing world where toys play while their owners are away. Woody is a pull-string cowboy doll and is the leader of the toys until the latest, greatest action figure, Buzz Lightyear, enters the picture. When the toy rivals are separated from their owner, they learn to put aside their differences and work as a team to get back home to the boy they love. 周五影院：《玩具总动员》

On display will be a combination of personally owned red, white, and blue quilts of all sizes plus over 100 service quilts made by OCQ Guild members which are being donated to the VA Hospital and the local Visiting Nurses Association for distribution to veterans.

candidates.title: Take A Guided Hike Through F.W. Kent Park with ICPL
candidates.description: At 1062-acres, F.W. Kent Park is the largest property owned by Johnson County Conservation Department. It contains a combination of relic and reconstructed prairies, oak savanna, forests, wetlands and a 27-acre lake. Join ICPL for a walking tour of a part of the park led by Iowa DNR District Forester Mark Vitosh and Johnson County Conservation staff from Kent Park to experience and insider's guide to the park as they point out things you might not see on your own. Kent park is located at 2048 Hwy 6, just 3 miles west of Tiffin. We'll start at 9am from the Conservation Education Center near in the northeast corner of the park about a mile in from the entrance. Follow the signs. Registration is required for this event due to limited parking. Only one person per vehicle needs to register. Please wear appropriate foot wear and clothing and bring drinking water and bug spray.

candidates.title: Iowa City Braver Angels monthly meeting
Latin American Music and Storytelling with Karin Stein
candidates.description: Join us this morning for a very special Storytime with Colombian musician Karin Stein. She will present a bilingual, family-friendly, music and storytelling program here at the library! Ms. Stein is part of a group called EcoMadres. EcoMadres is a group of mothers and fathers who fight for clean air and the health of our children. She will read the picture book, Señorita Mariposa, about the monarch butterfly migration based on an original, bilingual songs by Mister G. She will also share her passion and talent for Latin American music through an interactive concert to be enjoyed by the whole family! --- 今天的特别故事会我们将和来自哥伦比亚的音乐家卡琳·斯坦 (Karin Stein) 一起庆祝西班牙裔传统月。她将为大家带来适合全家参与的双语音乐故事节目！ Stein 女士同时也是EcoMadres 组织的成员，该组织由一群致力于为清洁空气和儿童健康而奋斗的家长们组成。 她将为我们朗读绘本Señorita Mariposa 《蝴蝶小姐》，这本书源自于G 先生的原创双语歌曲，讲述了帝王蝶迁徙的故事。她还将为所有家庭带来互动型音乐表演，分享她对拉丁美洲音乐的热爱和才华。 Únase a nosotros esta mañana para una hora de cuentos muy especial la música colombiana de Karin Stein. ¡Presentará un programa bilingüe, familiar, de música y narración de cuentos justo en el biblioteca! La Sra. Stein es parte de un grupo llamado EcoMadres. Ecomadres es un grupo de madres y padres que trabaja fuerte por un aire limpio y la salud de nuestros niños. Ella leerá el libro ilustrado, Señorita Mariposa, sobre la migración de la mariposa monarca basado en canciones originales bilingües de Mister G. ¡También compartirá su pasión y talento por la música latinoamericana a través de un concierto interactivo para que disfrute toda la famili

candidates.title: Art Play Sundays
candidates.venue: Iowa Children’s Museum, 1451 Coral Ridge Ave., Coralville, IA, 52241, United States
candidates.description: Creativity looks different every month in the Art Studio! These drop-in experiences invite kids and families to explore different artistic materials, experiment with new techniques, and create together through hands-on play. In addition to all of the art supplies you’ll typically find in our Art Studio, check out featured materials, collaborative projects, and artist-inspired activities. July Theme: Fiber Art Fun Weave, wrap, stitch, and create with a variety of fiber art materials, including yarn, ribbon, fabric, and string. Kids can contribute to a giant group weaving, practice beginner-friendly stitching techniques with plastic needles, and explore texture and pattern through hands-on making. Featured activities include collaborative weaving and wrapping all month long, inspired by artist Aluaiy Kaumakan, and nature weaving on the 12th

candidates.description: Join us in the Storytime Room for Summer Storytime on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday and Saturday mornings at 10:30am. The Storytime librarian will vary each week, and every day is open to all ages! Catch the all-ages Thrilling Thursday programs 10:00am in Meeting Room A. This is additional Storytime option to Stories in the Park at 9:30am on Mondays at Willow Creek Park, Wednesdays at Wetherby Park, and Fridays at Mercer Park! 暑期故事会将于每周一，二， 三，五, 六上午10：30在图书馆儿童活动室举行。 每周将由不同的工作人员带来不同的活动内容，欢迎所有年龄段的朋友参与！每周四上午10:00 将在会议室A举办Thrilling Thursday周四特别活动。 此外，图书馆还将于每周一，三，五上午9：30举办公园故事会，周一上午活动地点在 Willow Creek Park，周三在 Wetherby Park， 周五在 Mercer Park。 Únete a nosotros en la Sala de Cuentos para la Hora del Cuento de Verano los lunes, martes, miércoles y viernes por la mañana a las 10:30 am. ¡La bibliotecaria de la Hora del Cuento variará cada semana y todos los días están abiertos para todas las edades! ¡No te pierdas los emocionantes programas para todas las edades los jueves en un nuevo horario, a las 10:00 am en la Sala de Reuniones A! ¡Esta es una opción adicional para la Hora del Cuento en el Parque a las 9:30 am los lunes en el Parque Willow Creek, los miércoles en el Parque Wetherby y los viernes en el Parque Mercer!

candidates.title: Help Hours in the Digital Media Lab
candidates.description: Want some help starting a digital media project like scanning slides, editing a video, or converting a VHS tape? Expect to have questions while you use Adobe software? During Help Hours, a staff member can help you get started with a project, problem-solve struggles with our software and hardware, or just show you what's available in the Digital Media Lab. Individuals interested in the Senior Planet Computer Essentials class that was canceled may also drop in to work through the information at their own pace, with Stacey available for one-on-one instruction whenever demand allows. The Cricut, Elgato digital converters, and 8mm film converter can be reserved during this session. Go to icpl.org/DML to check availability and make your reservation. The inkjet printer is also available for drop-in printing. Note: Ordinary tech questions about using your devices or navigating a website can be asked anytime at the 2nd floor Info Desk.

candidates.title: Iowa City Math Circle Summer Camp - Math 2
candidates.description: The Iowa City Math Circle has been enriching students for 8 years. We are holding a free Math Camp for middle schoolers and high schoolers interested in preparing for math competitions, specifically AMC and AIME, as well as math beyond the school curriculum. We recommend a prerequisite of at least Algebra 1 to understand the material. Camp Schedule: Jul 6 - Jul 10: Math 1 Jul 13 - Jul 17: Math 2 Jul 20 - Jul 24: Coding 1 Jul 27 - Jul 31: Coding 2 Registration is required. Go to Iowa City Math Circle to register or if you have any questions. Snacks will be provided during every camp session. Join our instructors for lots of fun and learning this summer! Sponsored by the Iowa City Math Circle and Iowa City Public Library Teen Services.

Gilbert St, Iowa City. We will also dismiss from the Rec Center, but staff will be walking back to the library if parents and caregivers would like to pick up from the library.

You'll even get to swim home with your very own shark fin. Beat the heat. Join us for a shark-filled afternoon. Toothy grins guaranteed! 扮作鲨鱼游来游去，和你的朋友一起尽享夏日欢乐！我们将一起制作鲨鱼手工、玩鲨鱼游戏、享用鲨鱼造型的美味小吃，还要欢唱鲨鱼之歌。你还可以戴着自己做的的“鲨鱼鳍”游回家！和我们一起开怀欢笑，尽享这个夏日午后的清凉与乐趣吧。

You’ll also earn a sticker for each program you attend. Can’t make them all? Just stop by the ones you can make.

Certified instructor Julie Ellen will lead a FREE hour-long class every Monday at 12:30 PM. The attached PDF waiver must be completed prior to your first participation in Chair Yoga and updated annually. Copies are available at the Library or you may print ahead. candidates.title: Let's Talk Books: Fathers & Daughters
candidates.description: Relationships between fathers & daughters can run the spectrum from deeply connected and heartwarming to frought with issues at every turn. Fiction about fathers & daughters can be found in every genre - from general fiction to mysery, science fiction, or even westerns. Gather a few of your favorites and join us for the disscussion. Goodread's Fathers & Daughters list or Father Daughter Relationship lists or an ICPL catalog search for can give lost of inspiration. Not a traditional book group, Let's Talk Books is a series of casual online conversations about books. We meet on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month on Zoom to talk about our favorite books in a specific genre or on a theme that changes each time. We each bring a few titles to share, and by the end of the discussion we all have a few new titles on our to-be-read lists. Register here and you'll receive a confirmation email with the Zoom login details. You'll also get a reminder email a few days before the event. Everyone is welcome!

ON STANDS THIS WEEK — 25TH ANNIVERSARY ISSUE 🎉 "Had enough of Mega-this, Super-that and Big-whatever? Fed up with being treated like a dumbass by your local corporate newspaper? Say hello to the first issue of Little Village, your recipe for relief in 32 pages of words and pictures," reads a note from publisher @toddkimm55 in the July 2001 magazine. "Little Village favors the small and local, champions the sustainable, yet believes in considering more than one side of an issue ... Our goal is to start fires and put them out, to raise expectations and lower the limbo bar, to be serious and to be stupid. If you don't like one thing, you might like another." The more things change here at Little Village — longer issues, statewide coverage, a salaried staff of editors — the more things stay the same. We're still in print, still free, still local, ad-supported, independent, serious and stupid. In this issue: Kimm and other former LV publishers and editors reflect on a quarter century and 354 issues. Plus: Longtime LV music columnist @kembrew recalls his favorite interviews for Prairie Pop over the decades. Also: Ketamine therapy clinics are cropping up in Iowa as a unique UI study utilizing the drug gets underway, and owners of small venues are in agreement with audiences: concert ticket prices are too damn high.

Iowa may soon be back to swing-state status, according to ISU political science professor Karen Kedrowski. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 📸 @emmamclatch⁠ 🖋️ Kadin Luhmann/Iowa Capital Dispatch

This Sunday, the Iowa City Aerohawks (and guest pilots) will show off their tricks at their 33rd Annual Air Show. This may be your last chance to pull up a lawn chair and watch them at their current field, adjacent to the Iowa City Landfill just off IWV road, where they’ve taken to the skies since 1972. Rich DeVepo, who spoke with LV ahead of the event and for a story in LV’s April’s mag, is proud to be part of a club that invites all phases and walks of life, whether truck drivers or rocket scientists, emphasizing that all ages and genders may find interest in soaring to new heights.⁠ ⁠ DeVepo notes that while some people unexpectedly join the club on a drive to the landfill, drawn in by the sight of planes diving and twisting overhead, the Air Show is an excellent way to discover your interest in the hobby.⁠ ⁠ If you would like to bring a pop-up canopy for sun cover, DeVepo recommends getting there early. Otherwise, bleachers will be available, though you may need to bring a lawn chair, as the Aerohawks have welcomed as many as 2,000 attendees in previous years.⁠ ⁠ Find the full story at the link in our bio ✈️⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ William Lowell⁠ 📹️ @yeagernc

"That’s the strange alchemy of building something with the right people, in the right place, at the right time: you make choices and take risks, and then serendipity keeps handing you the collaborators and the moments that make those choices and risks pay off," said Alissa Hansen, former LV publisher. Read the retrospective at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get stories like these delivered directly to your inbox.⁠
Futile Wrath — July 2026 🍕⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ @sam_locke_ward

A poll from the New York Times finds Hinson ahead of Turek and Sand squeaking by Lahn. A poll by Fox News paints a very different picture. Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 🖊️ Paul Brennan
At the age of 90, Marcia Wegman is presenting what she is calling her final show, one that also serves as an homage to her time running a beloved retail store in the heart of Iowa City.

“Noncitizens are not bargaining chips to be shifted from district to district as litigation strategy,” the chief judge of the Southern District of Iowa warned ICE and DHS. "This pattern of behavior is troubling.” Read the story at the link in our bio, and sign up for LV newsletters to get the news delivered directly to your inbox.⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ Clark Kauffman / Iowa Capital Dispatch⁠ 📸 DHS

The Fourth of July holiday weekend brings a fresh batch of music, arts and culture events, along with the traditional firework affairs.⁠ ⁠ In Eastern Iowa, the Iowa City Jazz Festival returns this weekend, with Saturday’s programming running all the way up to the city’s fireworks display at 9:30 p.m. and continuing after with a jam session by @theeblakebass Quartet at Wilder.⁠ ⁠ In Cedar Rapids, the amapiano, Afrobeat and dancehall tunes will fill the Cultivate Hope Urban Farm for an AfroVibes Fourth of July Bash.⁠ ⁠ And in Central Iowa, the @desmoinessymphony holds their 32nd Annual Yankee Doodle Pops celebration at the Iowa State Capitol this Friday. Will the cannon fire scare folks like it scared “the ever living shit” out of our reviewer from last year? Most Likely!⁠ ⁠ Wherever you go this weekend, make sure to get plenty of water and look out for signs of heat exhaustion, as an extreme heat advisory extends through most of Central and Eastern Iowa.

As we look ahead to Yotopia’s 15th anniversary this September, we’ve found ourselves reflecting on the institutions that have shaped Iowa City into the place we love. This month, our beloved @littlevillagemag celebrates 25 years of independent journalism in our community. We had a blast joining the anniversary celebration—especially watching our very own Frannie Froyo make a splash in the dunk tank (disguised as our co-owner Ryan). We’ve been advertising with Little Village since Yotopia opened nearly 15 years ago because we believe strongly in their work. Like Little Village, we believe a free and independent press is essential to a healthy community, and that thoughtful journalism and arts reporting make Iowa a better place to live. Their mission—to improve the quality of life in our state through high-quality journalism and arts reporting—is one we’re proud to support. Subscribe or donate to LV through the link in our Bio! #iowacity #iowacitypride #journalismmatters #independentjournalism #localjournalism
This Modern World — July 2026⁠ ⁠ 🖋️ @realtomtomorrow

Ready to travel back in time to the golden era of rock? Mark your calendars for this Friday, July 10th, because Winterland is taking over the Ped Mall Stage for our Friday Night Concert Series! Specializing in the Grateful Dead's legendary catalog from 1969 through the late '70s, Winterland brings an uncanny authenticity, incredible musicianship, and a beautiful, high-energy connection to the crowd. Bring your friends, grab your lawn chairs, and get ready to dance the night away! A massive thank you to our wonderful presenting sponsor, McGrath Toyota of Iowa City, and our incredible stage co-sponsors, Bread Garden Market & Bakery, ReUnion Brewery, and Joseph's Steakhouse Iowa City, for supporting free live music in our community! #sotaic #sotaic26 #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #FridayNightConcertSeries #Winterland #GratefulDead #GraduateIowaCity #FreeEventsIC #DowntownIowaCity

TONIGHT IS THE NIGHT! We are heading to the park tonight! Grab your family, friends, and lawn chairs and meet us at the Terry Trueblood Recreation Area for Music on the Move featuring Hard Luck & the Troubles! Come out and enjoy their soulful, gritty Americana and blues-driven energy right here in Iowa City, fronted by the incredible lead vocals and piano of Roland Racevskis. It's the perfect way to spend a beautiful Wednesday summer evening! Time: 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM | Location: Terry Trueblood Recreation Area | Cost: FREE! We want to extend a special thank you to our wonderful band sponsor, @Navigate Homes, for supporting tonight's performance and helping keep the arts thriving in our community! See you all tonight! #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #MusicOnTheMove #HardLuckAndTheTroubles #NavigateHomes #IowaCityArts #FreeLiveMusic

Join us on July 9 for Eat for the Arts at 30hop Coralville! Whether you’re stopping in for lunch, grabbing a spot on the rooftop patio, or enjoying a family dinner, 15% of total sales for the day will be donated to Summer of the Arts. Your meal helps support free, family-friendly arts programming across our community! 30 HOP is a local favorite known for: - Modern Industrial Vibe & Rooftop Patio: The perfect atmosphere to relax with great views, excellent cocktails, and friendly service. - American Bites with an Asian Fusion Twist: A fantastic, unique menu with something for everyone. - Incredible Craft Beer Selection: Dozens of draft lines featuring the best local and regional brews. Eat well. Give back. Keep the arts thriving in our community! #sotaic26 #sotaic #30hopCoralville #IowaFoodie #EatForTheArts #SupportTheArts

Massive festivals like the 2026 Iowa Arts Festival and 2026 Iowa City Jazz Festival takes a village to build, but it also takes a lot of fuel to keep our incredible team running! We want to send a huge, heartfelt THANK YOU to the amazing local businesses that kept our staff and volunteers fed, energized, and ready to go all weekend long. Shoutout to these phenomenal downtown restaurants that generously kept our team fed: The Wedge Pizzeria, Nodo Downtown, Hamburg Inn No. 2, Oasis Street Food & Oasis Falafel Restaurant and Micky's Irish Pub & Grill! We are so grateful for your incredible community support and for keeping us well-fed through every single set! #sotaic26 #sotaic #IowaCityJazz #SupportLocal #CommunityPartners #DowntownIowaCity #SummerOfTheArts

A huge thank you to everyone who attended and supported our 2026 Jazz Brunch Fundraiser on Sunday, July 5th. Because of your incredible generosity, we can keep bringing world-class music and arts programming to our community! An extra special shoutout goes to the brilliant committee who organized all the magic behind the scenes: Lisa, Doris, and Cathy—thank you for your time, vision, and hard work! We also want to extend a beautiful thank you to Holly Jones and Growing Blooms for providing the absolutely gorgeous floral arrangements that brought the room to life. #sotaic26 #sotaic #IowaCityJazz #JazzBrunch #SupportTheArts #GrowingBlooms #CommunityStrong #SummerOfTheArts

COMING UP THIS WEEK! Get ready to spend your Wednesday evening with some incredible live music! Join us this Wednesday, July 8th, for another wonderful edition of Music on the Move. We’ll be out at the Terry Trueblood Recreation Area from 6:30 PM – 8:00 PM enjoying a free, family-friendly performance by Hard Luck & the Troubles! Led by the soulful vocals and piano of Roland Racevskis, this group brings a high-energy performance you won't want to miss. Pack up your lawn chairs, grab some blankets, and bring your favorite snacks for a perfect summer night in the park. A huge thank you to our amazing series Presenting Sponsor, Hills Bank, for making these free community events possible! #sotaic26 #IowaCity #SummerOfTheArts #MusicOnTheMove #LiveLocalMusic #HillsBank #FreeEventsIC #TerryTrueblood

There are only a few hours left of the 2026 Iowa City Jazz Festival, proudly presented by GreenState Credit Union! The music is flowing, the sun is shining, and the downtown vibes are perfect. You still have time to grab that one festival snack you've been thinking about all weekend, find a unique piece of art, and catch the final sets on stage. Don't let the weekend end without saying goodbye—get down here before we pack it all up at 4:00 PM! #sotaic26 #sotaic #SundayVibes #SupportTheArts #IowaCity
